title: Odyssey, Book 13: Return to Ithaca
description: Book 13 of Homer's Odyssey: Return to Ithaca.
introduction: Book 13 of the Odyssey: Return to Ithaca. The Greek text follows the standard Homeric vulgate.

translation.original_text: Ὣς ἔφαθ᾿, οἱ δ᾿ ἄρα πάντες ἀκὴν ἐγένοντο σιωπῇ.
translation.text: The Phaeacians brought Odysseus home to Ithaca while he slept. Poseidon turned their ship to stone. Athena met Odysseus on the shore and disguised him as an old beggar. She told him of the suitors and devised a plan for their destruction.
